docs.title: Configurable Backend Engine
docs.text: A Low/No Code Tool to build Backend Rest APIs by using building blocks.

docs.title: Introduction to Blocks
docs.text:
Blocks are the building pieces for building configurable backend routes. They connect one-by-one just like a lego brick.

Each block has a Source (blue knob) and Target (green knob) Connection(s), and some nodes have other Connection(s) as well which has different purposes.

Type Color Purpose Source Blue Connects to next node and optionally passes the input Target Green Accepts connection and input

docs.title: Built-in Blocks
docs.text:
The below 2 are the important blocks for the request.

  • Entrypoint: Default block which exists only one in any request. It is the starting point of the request, which takes care of parsing request body, query/route params, and headers.
  • Response: Block which returns the response to the http router.

There are several built-in blocks available. (Adding more every day)

  • If: Conditional If condition block.
  • Loops:
    • For: Loop block which loops from start to end with step (incrementer).
    • ForEach: Inherited by for loop, which accepts array as input or from params and loops through it.
  • HTTP
    • Get Header: Get header from the request.
    • Set Header: Set header to the response.
    • Get Cookie: Get cookie from the request.
    • Set Cookie: Set cookie to the response.
    • Get Param: Get route/query param from the request.
    • Get Body: Get body from the request.
  • JsRunner: Runs Javascript code in an isolated sandbox vm (uses 'node:vm' package) with the current request's context.
  • Transformer: Transforms the js object to a new object with specified field map.
  • SetVar: Block which sets variable to the current request's context. It is accessible throughout the request.
  • Response: Block which returns the response to the http router.
  • Logging
    • Console: Logs output to CLI console. (not much useful for a server-based, but useful for development purposes).

docs.title: Entrypoint Block
docs.text: This is the default and exists only one in any request. It is the starting point of the request, which takes care of parsing request body, query/route params, and headers.

docs.title: Connections
docs.text:
Type Purpose Source The body of the request will be passed to the next block

Note

All the request's metadata such as query params, route params, headers, and cookies will be available as a global variable. See request's context for more details.

docs.title: Response/Output Block
docs.text: This block is used to return the response to the router. Whatever data which is sent into the param block will be the body of that request.

docs.title: Connections
docs.text:
Type Purpose Target The body of the request must be passed from the previous block as an input to this block.

In addition to the body, it is also possible to set any headers to the request. To set any headers to the request, it is required to set the responseHttpHeaders variable in the context. Below is an example to set a header, but this is being run inside a JS Runner block.

responseHttpHeaders["Content-Type"] = "application/json";

Note

It is not required to initialize the responseHttpHeaders in vars, doing so will override the existing headers.

docs.title: The Request Context
docs.text:
The request context has access to the following:

  • Query Parameters: HTTP Query parameters such as /api/fruits?search=apple&color=green
  • Route Parameters: HTTP Route parameters such as /api/fruits/{fruitID}
  • Request Headers: HTTP Headers such as Authorization: Bearer <token>
  • Cookies: HTTP Cookies such as sessionID=123456
  • HTTP Method: HTTP Method such as GET, POST, PUT, DELETE
  • and more...

docs.title: Accessing the Context
docs.text:
All variables in the example such as httpRequestQuery is a global variable and can be accessed from Js Runner block.

  • Accessing the query parameter in Js Runner Block 
      const search = getQueryParam('search');
  const fruitId = getRouteParam('fruitID');
  const authToken = getHeader('Authorization')
  const sessionId = getCookie('sessionID');
  setCookie('sessionID', {
    value: '123456',
  });
  setHeader('Content-Type', 'application/json');
  const requestBody = getRequestBody(); // { "name": "Apple", "color": "Green" }
  const method = httpRequestMethod; // GET, POST, PUT, DELETE
  const route = httpRequestRoute; // /api/fruits/{fruitID}

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: Get up and running by cloning git repo.
